home.page_title: FreedInk - collective blogs with a shared byline
home.og_description: Groups of writers publish together on FreedInk. Every member's name is on the blog, only members can post, and no one can prove who wrote which piece.
home.subhead: This is a blogging platform that is a bit different than others. We aim to construct blogs shaped around like minds who want to speak more freely, but with some weight behind it.

blog.contributors_note: Any of these contributors could have written any post below — individual authorship is hidden.
blog.not_found_hint: Maybe the blog was deleted or there was a mistype?

members.title: Members of {blog}
members.heading: Members
members.note: Everyone who has joined this blog and could have written any post. Individual authorship is never revealed.

comments.heading: Comments
comments.anonymous_hint: Comments are anonymous: posting proves you're a member of this blog without revealing which one.
comments.sign_in_to_comment_suffix: to comment as a blog member.
comments.only_members: Only members of this blog can comment.

auth.invite_banner_signed_in: Hold on while we add you to the blog…
auth.invite_banner_signed_out: Sign in or create an account below — we'll add you to the blog right after.
auth.skip_to_dashboard: Skip and go to your dashboard

identity.intro: Welcome, {username}. Freed.Ink generates a Semaphore identity that lets you post anonymously inside blogs you're a member of. The secret part of that identity is encrypted with the password you pick below and stored in our database.
identity.forgot_warning: If you forget it, you'll have to rotate to a new identity (past posts stay attributed to the old one and remain verifiable).
identity.already_have: You already have an active identity. Use Settings → Rotate identity to change it.
identity.display_name_hint: Shown in the site navigation and in each blog's contributor list. You can change it any time in Settings. Leave it blank to use the auto-generated name.
identity.display_name_placeholder: How you'd like to be shown
identity.error_min_length: password must be at least 12 characters
identity.recovery_blurb: These 24 words regenerate your identity if you ever forget your password or lose this device. Write them down and store them offline. Anyone with this phrase can act as you.
identity.recovery_warning: FreedInk cannot show this phrase again and cannot recover it for you.
identity.recovery_ack: I have saved my recovery phrase somewhere safe.

admin.no_owned: You don't own any blogs yet.
admin.no_editing: You aren't an editor on any blogs yet.
admin.no_reviewing: You aren't a reviewer on any blogs yet.
admin.no_authoring: You aren't an author on any blogs yet.

settings.heading: Settings
settings.display_name_hint: This is the name shown in the site navigation and in each blog's contributor list. Leave it blank to fall back to your username.
settings.display_name_placeholder: How you'd like to be shown
settings.display_name_too_long: display name must be 80 characters or fewer
settings.devices_blurb: Each device you sign in on holds its own private key. Revoke a lost or unused device to drop it from every blog you can act in.
settings.device_revoke_confirm: Revoke this device? It will no longer be able to post, comment, or vote in any blog. This cannot be undone.
settings.device_last_active_hint: You can't revoke your last active device — enroll another first, or use “reset all devices”.
settings.rotate_identity_blurb: Generates a new Semaphore identity, revokes the active one, and refreshes the membership snapshot of every blog you belong to. Past proofs (votes, comments, posts) keep verifying against their original snapshot.
settings.revoke_confirm: Revoke this session? Any device using it will be signed out.
settings.data_rights_blurb_suffix: for full details on what these do (and what they don't — anonymous posts stay).
settings.deletion_explanation: This permanently deletes your account, sessions, passkeys, wallets, identity blobs, and blog memberships. Anonymous posts and comments remain (they are not linked to your account in our database).
settings.error_confirm_username: confirmation must equal your username exactly
settings.identity_rotated: identity rotated; snapshots updated for every blog you belong to
settings.rotate_password_error: pick a password ≥12 chars and confirm it
settings.rotate_confirm_prompt: Rotate identity? Past posts stay attributed to the old identity.
settings.restore_blurb: Forgot your password? Paste your 24-word recovery phrase to unlock your identity for this session, then set a new password below to make it permanent. Your identity and every blog membership stay the same.
settings.restore_ok: Identity unlocked for this session. Set a new password below to make it permanent.
settings.restore_mismatch: That recovery phrase does not match any identity on this account.
settings.restore_invalid: That does not look like a valid 24-word recovery phrase.
settings.restore_set_password_blurb: Re-encrypts your restored identity under a new password. Same identity, same memberships — only the password changes.
settings.restore_password_saved: New password set. Use it next time you unlock this identity.
settings.restore_password_error: pick a password ≥12 chars and confirm it
settings.restore_password_failed: Could not set the new password. Try restoring again.
